violations.text: Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machine was turned off and emptied during the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/02/2026

Establishment is not maintaining daily time as a public health control logs. Per approval letter, using time as a public health control is contingent on maintaining daily time as a public health control logs with all required information including: time made, how many, and how many discarded after 4hrs. New Violation. Correct By: 02/02/2026

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by droppings and gnawed insulation in the walk-in cooler. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Establishment is to continue working with pest control to minimize pest activity. New Violation. Correct By: 02/02/2026

chicken wings were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in cooler. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Food items were placed on a food storage rack during the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/02/2026

Utensils (gelato scoops and pizza slicers) were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. Utensils between uses shall be stored in one of the following manners: on a dry clean surface, in water that is maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or more, or in water with sufficient flow to flush particulates to the drain. Utensils were cleaned and sanitized during the time of inspection and were placed on a dry clean surface. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/02/2026

The floor grout and tiles were observed cracked in the kitchen area was observed to be low and is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/30/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the deli slicer and ice machine have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned at a frequency to prevent the accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 05/14/2025

The ice machine is lacking a proper air gap, an air break was observed. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed New Violation. Correct By: 05/14/2025

The floor grout and tiles were observed cracked in the kitchen area was observed to be low and is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 10/30/2025
The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2025

An open employees' beverage container was observed stored in a food preparation area. Employees shall not drink from open beverage containers in order to prevent contamination of their hands, the container, and exposed food. Beverage was removed from food preparation area. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2025
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen by the dishwasher was lacking paper towels. Paper towels were provided during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2025

Molluscan shellfish tags must be maintained for 90 days in chronological order and the date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Deli slicer is to be disassembled, cleaned and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. The ice machine was turned off and emptied during time of inspection. Ice machine is to be cleaned, sanitized, and allowed to air dry prior to putting equipment back in service. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2025

Various food items were observed held at an ambient temperature of 46-50 degrees Fahrenheit within the prep unit by the fryolator. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food items were evaluated and voluntarily disposed of during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2025

Garlic in oil was observed held at ambient room temperature of 75 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food items were evaluated and disposed of during time of inspection. Proper storage methods discussed with PIC during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2025

Pickled Vegetables which are prepared within the establishment were observed past their date of disposition. Food items prepared within the establishment shall be disposed of after 7 days including the date of preparation. Food items were voluntarily disposed of during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2025

The establishment was observed using time as a public health control (TPHC) for slices of pizza with various toppings. Establishment does not have prior approval from RIDOH Center for Food Protection for using TPHC for pizza slices. Establishment is to cease practice. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2025

The door to the outside, located in the storage area and the door by the dining room, are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 05/30/2025

The hand sink by the raw bar unit was lacking a splash guard to prevent potential contamination of single service take-out containers. Splash guard is to be installed. New Violation. Correct By: 05/30/2025

Dough trays were observed stored on-top of a trash can during preparation. No food containers shall be stored on refuse containers. Food items were transferred into clean storage container and contaminated storage container was cleaned and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2025

Utensils (Pizza Cutters) were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. Utensils shall be stored in one of the following manners: on a dry clean surface, in water of 135 degrees Fahrenheit or more, or in water with sufficient flow to flush particulates to the drain. Utensils were cleaned and sanitized then moved to a dry clean surface. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2025
Refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair on the prep unit by the fryolator. Refrigerator door gaskets shall be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 05/30/2025

The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quaternary ammonium sanitizing solution for the 3-bay sink. Establishment is to obtain test strips. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the walk-in cooler gasket, fan guards, floors around, shelving next to and sides of fryolator have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-food contact surfaces shall be cleaned at a frequency necessary to prevent the accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2025

The ice machine is lacking a proper air gap, an air break was observed. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. New Violation. Correct By: 05/14/2025

The condensate from the pizza prep unit by preparation space refrigeration unit was observed accumulating within the unit and overflowing into the low boy portion of the unit. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain or other means of conveyance. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2025
The receptacle in the women's toilet room is lacking a cover. A toilet room used by females shall be provided with a covered receptacle for sanitary napkins. New Violation. Correct By: 05/14/2025

The floor grout and tiles were observed cracked in the kitchen area was observed to be low and is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 10/30/2025

Staff failed to sanitize utensils (knives) after washing and rinsing them. After being cleaned, equipment and utensils must be sanitized in the 3-bay-sink or dishwasher. Knives were washed and sanitized at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/28/2023

Working containers (2 spray bottles at Bar) used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. PIC removed bottles at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/28/2023
